title: Ghazvin (Qazvin) Area Development Project -- Project Performance Audit: Original Plan vs. Actual Outcomes (World Bank, 1978)
source_org: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development (World Bank), Operations Evaluation Department (OED)
source_url: https://openknowledge.worldbank.org (World Bank Archives, Iran country documents)
extraction_method: MANUAL TRANSCRIPTION from data/raw/world-bank-archives-iran/historical-documents/1978_ghazwin_development_project_completion.pdf (58 pages, already present in archive, not previously mined). Text extracted via `pdftotext -layout`; the 'Project Performance Audit Basic Data Sheet' (Sections A-D) and the narrative 'Highlights' rendered cleanly with no column-scrambling -- no PNG visual-verification pass needed. Figures transcribed exactly as printed, including the report's own footnoted caveats (e.g. footnote /e on a much higher now-estimated actual cost for follow-on works).
time_coverage: 1962 (Bank conception) - 1977 (audit basic-data-sheet as-of date), auditing a project appraised 1967 and loan-closed 1974
frequency: single project audit (not a time series)
license_terms: World Bank Archives — public disclosure authorized (restricted-distribution document, historical)
notes: Companion dataset to wb1967ghazvin-project-cost-and-parameters (the original appraisal). This is a genuinely rare find in this project's archive: an ex-post World Bank performance audit comparing appraised targets to actual results, rather than just a forward-looking appraisal. Key finding: economic rate of return collapsed from an appraised 10% to approximately 0%, and only US$9.2M of the original US$22M loan was disbursed (US$12.8M cancelled) -- attributed to overestimated groundwater availability, the Borrower's rejection of an initial understanding to expropriate private wells for the project under land reform, and Bank/Borrower disagreements over procurement procedures. The report also references the Dez project directly (footnote /d: joint Bank supervision missions split field time 50/50 between Dez and Ghazvin), reinforcing that these two document sets are companions within the same World Bank Iran water-sector appraisal series. Part of this project's systematic Pahlavi-era World Bank PDF mining effort (Round 38).
